The authentication token is stored securely in the client-side storage mechanism which provides encrypted access to the
user credentials and session information that must be validated on every single request to the protected application
programming interface endpoints which enforce strict role-based access control policies defined by the security
team and reviewed quarterly to ensure compliance with the latest industry standards and regulatory requirements that
govern how personally identifiable information is collected stored processed and transmitted across network boundaries
including but not limited to encryption at rest and in transit using approved cryptographic algorithms and key
management practices that follow the principle of least privilege and separation of duties as recommended by the
National Institute of Standards and Technology guidelines for secure software development lifecycle practices which
mandate regular security assessments penetration testing code reviews and vulnerability scanning across all production
environments and development pipelines to identify and remediate potential security weaknesses before they can be
exploited by malicious actors who continuously probe internet-facing services for known vulnerabilities and misconfigurations
